description: Autism Spectrum Disorder survey-based prediction model. Requires M-CHAT survey responses and demographic information.

A1_Score.description: Does the child look at you when you call his/her name?

A2_Score.description: How easy is it for you to get eye contact with the child?

A3_Score.description: Does the child point with one finger to show you something interesting?

A4_Score.description: Does the child bring toys to you and/or bring you things they have found?

A5_Score.description: Do you think your child understands what you say?

A6_Score.description: When something unusual happens, does the child look at your face to see your reaction?

A7_Score.description: Does the child like movement activities (e.g., being swung, bounced on your knee)?

A8_Score.description: Does the child imitate you (e.g., you make a face - does your child imitate it)?

A9_Score.description: Does the child say any words on his/her own?

A10_Score.description: Does the child repeat words after you?

extraction_example.raw_text: M-CHAT Screening Form (English). Child Age: 36 months. Gender: Male. Ethnicity: Hispanic. Jaundice at Birth: Yes. Relation of Respondent: Parent. Prior Screening App: Yes. Family History of Autism: Yes. Q1: Does the child look at you when you call his/her name? Yes. Q2: How easy is it for you to get eye contact? No. Q3: Does the child point with one finger? Yes. Q4: Does child bring toys to show you? No. Q5: Does child understand what you say? Yes. Q6: When unusual happens, does child look at your face? No. Q7: Does child like movement activities? Yes. Q8: Does child imitate you? No. Q9: Does child say any words? Yes. Q10: Does child repeat words? No.
